description: Video recommendation algorithm based on readiness assessment results for Chapter 4: Thousands Around Us (place value up to 5 digits)

excellent_performance.rationale: Student has excellent understanding of 3-digit place value and comparison, can fast-track to 5-digit numbers with challenge content

videoSequence.reason: Introduction to place value up to 5 digits
Learn to read and write 4 and 5 digit numbers
Compare and order numbers up to 5 digits
Estimation and rounding of large numbers
Challenge: Practice all forms of 5-digit numbers

ready_performance.rationale: Student is ready, provide complete coverage of place value and large number concepts

videoSequence.reason: Introduction to place value up to 5 digits
Learn to read and write large numbers
Compare and order numbers up to 5 digits
Estimation and rounding of large numbers

partial_readiness.rationale: Student has gaps in 3-digit place value or counting, needs targeted review before tackling 5-digit numbers

adaptiveLogic.description: Select prerequisite videos based on specific weak concepts

exampleSequence.reason: Start main topic: Place value up to 5 digits

not_ready.rationale: Student needs significant prerequisite review in 3-digit place value and counting before attempting 5-digit numbers

videoSequence.reason: Begin main content: Reading large numbers

postPlaylistAction.message: After watching these prerequisite videos, retake the readiness quiz to check if you are ready to continue with larger numbers.

conceptBasedRemediation.description: Map specific weak concepts to remedial videos

counting_1000.conceptName: Counting forward and backward up to 1000

number_sense.conceptName: General number sense and understanding of number magnitude

comprehensionCheckpoints.description: Quick checks during video watching to adjust playlist

questions.question: In the number 42,356, what is the value of the digit 2?
questions.explanation: The digit 2 is in the thousands place. So its value is 2,000.

questions.question: Which place comes after the thousands place (moving left)?
questions.explanation: Moving left, the places are: ones, tens, hundreds, thousands, ten thousands.

ifAllCorrect.message: Excellent! You understand 5-digit place value well.

ifAllWrong.reason: Student struggling with place value, review 3-digit place value

questions.question: Which is greater: 34,567 or 34,576?
questions.explanation: Both have the same ten thousands, thousands, and hundreds digits. In the tens place, 7 > 6, so 34,576 is greater.

ifCorrect.reason: Student ready for challenge problems with all forms

ifWrong.message: Watch the comparing numbers video again, then try some practice problems.

skip_ahead.action: Skip practice video, jump to challenge or next topic

add_support.action: Pause main topic, insert prerequisite review on 3-digit place value

suggest_break.trigger: After 4 videos watched in a single session
suggest_break.action: Suggest taking a 5-minute break before continuing

recommend_practice.trigger: Student completes videos but scores < 70% on checkpoint
recommend_practice.action: Recommend practising place value with an abacus or place value chart at home

recommend_retake.trigger: Student in not_ready category completes all prerequisite videos
recommend_retake.action: Strongly recommend retaking readiness assessment before main content

high_score.message: Excellent! You know your 3-digit place value and counting really well. You are ready to learn about thousands and 5-digit numbers. We have created a fast-track playlist with challenge content for you.

good_score.message: Good work! You understand 3-digit numbers well. Let's explore the world of thousands and bigger numbers with complete coverage of all topics.

partial_score.message: You have some gaps in place value or counting. Don't worry! We will start with targeted review videos on the concepts you need, then move to bigger numbers.

low_score.message: You need to review 3-digit place value and counting first. We have created a playlist focused on building a strong foundation. After watching, you can retake the quiz and then learn about thousands.

conceptProgressionMap.description: Shows how place value concepts build on each other

playlistCompletion.description: % of students who watch >= 80% of recommended videos

practiceQuizPassRate.description: % of students who score >= 70% on practice quiz after watching playlist

videoRelevanceRating.description: Average student rating (out of 5) for playlist helpfulness

timeToMastery.description: Average minutes from readiness quiz to practice quiz pass

placeValueFluency.description: % of students who can correctly identify place value of any digit in a 5-digit number

numberComparisonAccuracy.description: % of students who can correctly compare and order 5-digit numbers

implementationNotes.recommendedValidation: Test with 15-20 Grade 4 students across all readiness levels

expectedOutcomes.excellent_students: Complete in 40-50 minutes with 90-100% practice quiz score
expectedOutcomes.ready_students: Complete in 35-40 minutes with 80-90% practice quiz score
expectedOutcomes.partial_students: Complete in 50-55 minutes with 70-80% practice quiz score (after prerequisite review)
expectedOutcomes.not_ready_students: Require prerequisite review (20 min) + retake + main content (25 min) = 45-55 total minutes, expected 60-75% on practice quiz

commonChallenges.challenge1: Students confusing face value and place value
commonChallenges.solution1: Emphasise that the position of a digit determines its value. Use a place value chart with columns labelled.
commonChallenges.challenge2: Students struggling with the transition from 3-digit to 4-digit and 5-digit numbers
commonChallenges.solution2: Use base-10 blocks: 10 hundreds make a thousand, 10 thousands make a ten thousand. Build numbers physically.
commonChallenges.challenge3: Students misreading large numbers or placing commas incorrectly
commonChallenges.solution3: Practice reading numbers aloud. Use the Indian system: ones, thousands, lakhs. Place commas after the hundreds and thousands periods.
